channel.title: Naija Digest Online
channel.description: Nigeria’s one-stop hub for breaking news, tech, football, celebrity gossip, Naija music, live TV, exchange rates and trending topics.

item.title: Peter Obi seeks probe of Enugu Air runway incident
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Peter Obi calls for a comprehensive investigation into the Enugu Air runway excursion at Benin Airport, emphasizing aviation safety and transparency.
Read More: https://punchng.com/peter-obi-seeks-probe-of-enugu-air-runway-incident/

item.title: Indian activist ends 26-day hunger strike over exam leaks
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Indian activist Sonam Wangchuk ends a 26-day hunger strike over exam leaks, demanding Education Minister’s resignation. Protests continue across India.
Read More: https://punchng.com/indian-activist-ends-26-day-hunger-strike-over-exam-leaks/

item.title: Trump weighs ‘massive attack’ on Iran after two weeks of consecutive strikes
item.dc:creator: Premium Times
item.description: President Donald Trump also said he is considering a “massive attack” on Iran, which would leave the country devastated. The post Trump weighs ‘massive attack’ on Iran after two weeks of consecutive strikes appeared first on Premium Times Nigeria.

item.title: Woman allegedly pours hot water on pregnant co-wife, newborn dies in Bauchi
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: The Bauchi State Police Command has arrested a 20-year-old woman, Rabi Abubakar, for allegedly pouring hot water on her pregnant co-wife during a domestic dispute, an attack that reportedly led to the death of a newborn baby. The suspect, a resident of Daba Village in Bauchi Local Government Area, w

item.title: South Africa: South Africa Dismisses Ghanaian Petition to ICC Over Migrant Violence
item.dc:creator: AllAfrica
item.description: [allAfrica] South Africa has dismissed as "opportunistic" a petition filed at the International Criminal Court by two Ghanaian nationals seeking an investigation into attacks on foreign migrants, arguing the country's legal system is capable of addressing such cases.

item.title: Troops, hunters capture five suspected kidnappers in Yobe
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Troops of the Joint Task Force North East, Operation HADIN KAI, in collaboration with the Yobe State Hunters Group, have arrested five suspected kidnappers during a coordinated raid operation in Fune Local Government Area of Yobe State. Security expert, Zagazola Makama, disclosed this in a post on X

item.title: Weather tracker: more than 900 wildfires rage across Canada
item.dc:creator: The Guardian
item.description: Fires remain out of control in Northwest Territories and Ontario, while Argentina endures record night-time heatRecent hot, dry weather in Canada has triggered hundreds of wildfires across the country. As of Wednesday, more than 900 were burning, scorching about 3m hectares of land. While fires have

item.title: Kano govt declares ‘Daba’ illegal, orders crackdown on practice
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: The Kano State Executive Council has declared the practice popularly known as “Daba” a deviant and unacceptable social act, directing security agencies and relevant government institutions to intensify enforcement against the practice across the state. The decision was among key policy r

item.title: Accord Party Elders Set Committee to Resolve Crisis
item.dc:creator: ThisDay
item.description: Chuks Okocha in Abuja The political crisis brewing within the Accord Party (AP) in Osun State has deepened as elders and stakeholders loyal to the party’s presidential candidate, Dr. Gbenga Olawepo-Hashim,

item.title: Compound hygiene: How to disinfecting domestic water tanks, cisterns after rain ingress
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: During the rainy season in Nigeria, heavy storms often lead to flooding and surface runoff entering poorly sealed household water tanks and underground cisterns. The post Compound hygiene: How to disinfecting domestic water tanks, cisterns after rain ingress appeared first on Tribune Online.

item.description:

item.title: Man Utd bring in Leicester goalkeeper Leitzig
item.dc:creator: BBC Sport Football
item.description: Manchester United sign goalkeeper Janina Leitzig from Leicester City for an undisclosed fee.

item.description: Most conversations about crypto adoption still center on price. Fewer center on plumbing. The settlement layers, stable units…

item.title: Wise Group shares tank after U.S. bank charter rejected. What’s next for the fintech.
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: Wise Group shares fell as much as 11% on Friday as the U.K.-headquartered, U.S.-listed fintech had its application for a national bank charter denied.

item.title: ‘77% of Nigerian Women Bleach Their Skin’: Beverly Naya Reflects On Documentary’s Impact.
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Nollywood actress and filmmaker Beverly Naya has opened up about the inspiration behind her acclaimed documentary on skin bleaching and colourism, saying the project was created to spark conversations about identity, beauty standards and the harmful effects of colourism in Nigeria.   Speaking d

item.description: Gbenga Sodeinde in Ado Ekiti The Ekiti State Government has hailed the federal government over the National Boundary Commission (NBC) resolve to mediate in the lingering border dispute between Eda Oniyo

item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: The ruthless labor history of your favorite fast food joint.

item.title: Doyle: Masquerade Ball can clinch historic King George win
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Sky Sports Racing ambassador Hollie Doyle reveals her thoughts on the King George at Ascot on Saturday and runs the rule over the best of the other weekend action at Ascot and York.

item.title: Why Newcastle are looking to emulate German giants on recruitment
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: When the Saudi PIF takeover of Newcastle was completed in late 2021 the new ownership set about adding age and experience to their newly acquired investment.

item.title: Odd Lots: How Franchising Led to the Gig Economy (Podcast)
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: When the fast food industry began booming in the 1950s, it did so via a new business model known as the franchise. This model allowed independent operators to license trademarks from a business like McDonald’s or Dunkin Donuts, and it soon spread across the country, with huge consequences for how Am

item.title: From aid campaigner to Africa minister: Can Kirsty McNeill rebuild Britain’s standing on the continent?
item.dc:creator: The Africa Report
item.description: The former Save the Children executive spent more than two decades pushing Britain to lead on global development from outside government. Now, as the country’s new minister for development and Africa, she must convince African partners that London still has the ambition and the credibility to do so.

item.title: Nigerian military wipes out ISWAP fighters in deadly Borno airstrike
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: The Air Component of Operation HADIN KAI has carried out a precision airstrike on a suspected Islamic State West Africa Province, ISWAP, hideout in the Southern Lake Chad region, killing several terrorists during a late-night operation in Borno State. The development was reported by Zagazola Makama,

item.description: The Nigeria Democratic Congress, NDC, presidential running mate, Rabiu Kwankwaso, has disclosed that he never collected one naira from the security vote while he was governor of Kano State for eight years. Kwankwaso made the remark while insisting that state police is due to a huge failure of leader

item.description: Governor Alex Otti of Abia State has distanced the South-East from Governor Hope Uzodinma's endorsement of President Bola Tinubu for a second term, insisting that the Imo State governor expressed only his personal opinion and cannot speak on behalf of the region or its people. The post Tinubu’

item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: THE Nigerian Safety Investigation Bureau (NSIB) has commenced an investigation into a runway excursion involving an Enugu Air Embraer E170 aircraft at Benin Airport (BNI). The post NSIB begins investigation into Enugu Air mishap at Benin airport appeared first on Tribune Online.

item.title: Khazanah Is Said to Sell $340 Million Tenaga Stake in Placement
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Malaysian sovereign wealth fund Khazanah Nasional Bhd. has sold 1.39 billion ringgit ($340 million) worth of shares in state-owned power firm Tenaga Nasional Bhd., according to people with knowledge of the matter.

item.title: Nigerians may pay more for fuel as global oil prices rise above $100 per barrel
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: NIGERIANS may pay more for fuel as global oil prices rose above $100 per barrel mark for the first time since May. The post Nigerians may pay more for fuel as global oil prices rise above $100 per barrel appeared first on Tribune Online.

item.title: Chinese Banks Break With Lending Benchmark in Pricing New Credit
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Three of the biggest Chinese banks experimented with anchoring new loans to interbank rates, a step toward a new pricing mechanism that could better reflect supply and demand for money in the market.

item.description:

item.title: Malaysia May Again Host Formula One Racing as Iran War Puts Bahrain in Doubt
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Malaysia could host a Formula One race for the first time in nine years, potentially replacing the Bahrain Grand Prix as the Middle East conflict drags on, according to the country’s Youth and Sports Minister Mohammed Taufiq Johari.

item.title: Editorial: Beyond life sentences, Nigeria must hunt down the terror masters
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: Justice is not measured merely by arrests. It is rather measured by lawful convictions secured through credible evidence and fair trials. The recent life sentences prove that Nigeria possesses the institutional capacity to achieve this objective.

item.dc:creator: Nairametrics

item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: A proposed merger between Iyogin Holdings Inc. and Ehime Bank Ltd. adds to a series of recent M&A activity between Japanese regional lenders as an aging and shrinking population weighs on their business.

item.description: Imposed citing forced labour concerns, the levies replace a temporary global duty brought in after the US Supreme Court struck down tariffs in February.

item.dc:creator: Nairametrics
item.description: Patreon is laying off 20% of its workforce, affecting about 93 employees, as the creator platform restructures amid changing market conditions and a broader wave of workforce adjustments across the technology industry. item.description: The actor’s brothers, Kevin and Derrick Boseman, have asked a Los Angeles court to remove his widow, Taylor Simone Ledward, as administrator of his estate.

item.title: Anambra offers plea bargain to criminal suspects, excludes murder, kidnapping cases
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: By Vincent Ujumadu AWKA — The Anambra State Government has introduced a plea bargain option for suspects undergoing criminal trial as part of efforts to accelerate the administration of justice and reduce delays in the prosecution of cases. However, suspects standing trial for murder, rape, armed ro

item.title: Indian activist Wangchuk ends 26-day hunger strike
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: Indian activist Sonam Wangchuk has ended a 26-day hunger strike over leaked medical entrance exams

item.title: Thousands Of Argentina Fans Sign Petition Calling For World Cup 2026 Final Replay
item.dc:creator: Completesports
item.description: Thousands of furious Argentina supporters are calling for the FIFA World Cup Final to be replayed following what they describe as a “corrupt” officiating display, beIN Sports reports. Argentina fell 1-0 to Spain in extra time of the tournament showpiece in New Jersey on Sunday. Ferran To

item.title: TikTok Steps Up Safety Drive, Deletes 4.8m Nigerian Videos in Q1
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Nigeria, one of TikTok’s quickest-growing African markets, saw 4.8 million user videos taken down in Q1 2026 amid the platform’s intensified push to curb misinformation, harmful material, and policy infractions The latest Community Guidelines Enforcement Report showed that Nigeria record

item.title: The PImpl idiom and the C++26 std:indirect type
item.dc:creator: Hacker News
item.description: Article URL: https://mariusbancila.ro/blog/2026/07/23/the-pimpl-idiom-and-the-cpp26-stdindirect-type/ Comments URL: https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=49032095 Points: 23 # Comments: 16

item.title: Ruiz, Gavi Gifted 150kg Of Tomatoes After Winning World Cup 2026
item.dc:creator: Completesports
item.description: Spanish duo Fabián Ruiz and Gavi have received their body weight in local tomatoes from their hometown of Los Palacios y Villafranca after winning the 2026 FIFA World Cup 2026. The two World Cup winners returned to the town, located south of Seville, on Wednesday for a civic reception at the town ha

item.title: Senate Leadership Meets Tinubu at Presidential Villa
item.dc:creator: TVC News
item.description: The leadership of the Senate on Thursday held a meeting with President Bola Tinubu at the Presidential Villa in Abuja to discuss issues of national importance aimed at strengthening governance and deepening collaboration between the executive and legislative arms of government. The meeting was discl

item.title: Anthony Joshua says tragic deaths of close friends continue to hurt ahead of comeback fight
item.dc:creator: Premium Times
item.description: Ahead of his comeback, Joshua admitted he has deliberately immersed himself in work because he is not emotionally ready to fully process the loss The post Anthony Joshua says tragic deaths of close friends continue to hurt ahead of comeback fight appeared first on Premium Times Nigeria.

item.title: US Slams 12.5% Tariff on Nigerian Imports over Forced‑labour Concerns
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The United States has lengthened the list of trade penalties against Nigeria by imposing a 12.5 per cent tariff on most Nigerian imports over allegations linked to forced labour. The new measure is part of a wider action against 60 economies that Washington says have not effectively banned the impor

item.title: NCAA threatens to suspend Royal Air Maroc operations over infractions
item.dc:creator: Nairametrics
item.description: The Nigerian Civil Aviation Authority (NCAA) has threatened to suspend Royal Air Maroc's operations in Nigeria over persistent passenger complaints and repeated consumer protection infractions. The post NCAA threatens to suspend Royal Air Maroc operations over infractions appeared first on Nairametr

item.title: South Africa’s most important crime witness isn’t human. It’s a smartphone.
item.dc:creator: TechCabal
item.description: Every WhatsApp conversation, location ping, cloud backup and piece of metadata can help investigators reconstruct relationships that might once have remained hidden.

item.title: Dangote Cement eyes London secondary listing to bypass Dubai’s delays
item.dc:creator: BusinessDay
item.description: ​Dangote Cement Plc has selected London for its upcoming secondary listing because executing share sales on the British exchange will

item.title: South Africa, Botswana, Zimbabwe Officially Submit Joint Bid To Host AFCON 2028
item.dc:creator: Completesports
item.description: South Africa, Botswana and Zimbabwe have officially submitted a joint bid to host the 2028 Africa Cup of Nations (AFCON), as the Southern African region seeks to bring the continent’s biggest football tournament back to the area. The ambitious proposal was confirmed by Botswana Football Association
